concerns.text: Task T009 depends on `data/raw/conversations.jsonl` as input, but no upstream task exists to acquire, download, or generate this specific file. The implementer cannot execute this task deterministically without a defined data source task.

Task T000 requires an 'assumed effect size (f²) from literature' to run power analysis but provides no task to locate, cite, or document this value. This leaves a critical parameter undefined, making the task non-deterministic and violating Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy).

Phase N+1 tasks (T037-T042) require creating new columns (`trust_immediate`, `authenticity_retrospective`, `noise_index`) in `data/processed/ratings.csv`. However, T005 and T005a strictly validate against a schema expecting only `authenticity_score`. The tasks lack a step to update the schema contracts or the validation logic, guaranteeing a runtime failure when T005a runs against the new data structure.

Task T001d references 'Human Matches' derived from a `gold_labels` column, but T001c defines the output columns as `conversation_id`, `text_content`, `authenticity_score`, `rater_id`, and `timestamp`. The column name mismatch (`gold_labels` vs `authenticity_score`) makes the task description ambiguous and unexecutable without guessing the correct column name.

Task T031 requires running the pipeline on a 'sample of conversations' to verify the 6-hour runtime constraint (SC-004). Since no task acquires the raw dataset (`conversations.jsonl`), the implementer has no concrete data to run this performance test against, rendering the task unexecutable.
